title: Race conditions and synchronization deterministic draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for Race conditions and synchronization; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Race conditions and synchronization: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Declaring a counter volatile makes counter++ atomic and therefore protects every invariant that includes the counter.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. volatile does not make compound actions atomic; confine mutable state, use an appropriate atomic operation, or synchronize the entire invariant transition.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. volatile does not make compound actions atomic; confine mutable state, use an appropriate atomic operation, or synchronize the entire invariant transition.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: volatile does not make compound actions atomic; confine mutable state, use an appropriate atomic operation, or synchronize the entire invariant transition.
Declaring a counter volatile makes counter++ atomic and therefore protects every invariant that includes the counter.

answer.explanation: volatile does not make compound actions atomic; confine mutable state, use an appropriate atomic operation, or synchronize the entire invariant transition.
